biography: Wilhelm Rontgen was a German physicist who discovered X-rays in 1895, revolutionizing medical diagnosis. He received the first Nobel Prize in Physics in 1901 and refused to patent his discovery, ensuring its free use for medical purposes worldwide.
aiSummary: Wilhelm Rontgen discovered X-rays in 1895, revolutionizing medical imaging. First Nobel Prize in Physics (1901). Refused to patent X-rays for public benefit. Based in Munich, Germany.

timeline.title: Discovered X-rays
timeline.description: Observed a new type of radiation that could pass through solid objects and image bones

timeline.title: Published X-ray findings
timeline.description: Published 'On a New Kind of Rays' which transformed medical diagnostics

timeline.title: First Nobel Prize in Physics
timeline.description: Received the inaugural Nobel Prize in Physics for X-ray discovery
